records.ety: [OE. trisen; of Scand. or Low German origin; cf. Sw. trissa a sheave, pulley, triss a spritsail brace, Dan. tridse a pulley, tridse to haul by means of a pulley, to trice, LG. trisse a pulley, D. trijsen to hoist.]

defs: To pull; to haul; to drag; to pull away.
quotes: Out of his seat I will him trice.

defs: To haul and tie up by means of a rope.

records.ety: [Sp. tris the noise made by the breaking of glass, an instant, en un tris in an instant; probably of imitative origin.]

defs: A very short time; an instant; a moment; -- now used only in the phrase in a trice.
quotes: A man shall make his fortune in a trice.
